channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The U.S. will resume Ukraine military aid and intelligence, as Kyiv approves ceasefire
item.description: The United States said it will resume sending military aid and intelligence to Ukraine, as Ukraine agreed to a Trump administration proposal for a monthlong ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis, Michele Kelemen, and Eleanor Beardsley, MPR News

item.title: Appeals court won't lift block on Trump's executive order attempting to end birthright citizenship
item.description: A third appellate court has upheld a block on President Donald Trump’s executive order limiting birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some Republican lawmakers have concerns about Elon Musk and DOGE. Here's what they've said
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans, particularly those in Congress, have been overwhelmingly supportive of broad cuts across the federal government at the hands of Elon Musk ’s Department of Government Efficiency. But some have had words of caution.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ official says she was dismissed over Mel Gibson's gun rights
item.description: "Decisions are being made based on relationships and loyalty, not based on facts or expertise or sound analysis,” Oyer said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: High-profile Dem jumps into crowded California governor’s race amid past office controversies
item.description: Former Rep. Katie Porter of Orange County is the latest top-name Democrat to join a crowded field for California's 'jungle' election for governor, to replace Gavin Newsom.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USDA halts more than $1B in funding for local food banks, schools
item.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) says it has nixed more than $1 billion in funding for local food banks and schools by terminating two programs that aid state, tribal and territorial government food purchases.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Canada electricity tariffs to target 3 US states
item.description: "If the United States escalates, I will not hesitate to shut the electricity off completely," said Doug Ford.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: China 'ripping off' American businesses – but the DOJ can fight them, GOP lawmaker says
item.description: Rep. Ashley Hinson is reintroducing a bill aimed at tackling China's trade crimes and establishing a DOJ task force focused on investigating and prosecuting them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Judge Amir Ali? The Biden-appointed federal judge at the center of Trump's USAID battle
item.description: The federal judge presiding over a challenge to the Trump administration's pause on USAID spending, U.S. District Judge Amir Ali, is a new face on the bench appointed by President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: First on Fox: Trump voter dons MAGA hat in warning to House Republicans against Medicaid cuts
item.description: A self-identified nursing home employee and lifelong Republican who voted for President Donald Trump is urging House GOP lawmakers to avoid making cuts to Medicaid, as he stars in a new seven-figure ad blitz by a pro-Democrat outside group as part of its "Hands off Medicaid" campaign.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Republican-led effort to halt climate change lawsuits in Democratic-led states
item.description: The Supreme Court has rejected a lawsuit from Republican attorneys general in 19 states aimed at blocking climate change suits against the oil and gas industry from Democratic-led states, including Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: With deadline looming Speaker predicts GOP can pass spending bill without Democrats
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., told reporters on Tuesday that he believes Republicans will be able to pass a partisan spending bill without the help of Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh and Claudia Grisales, MPR News

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Katie Porter jumps into California governor's race
item.description: Former Rep. Katie Porter (D-Calif.) announced her bid for California governor Tuesday, joining a growing field of Democratic contenders in the race to succeed Gov. Gavin Newsom (D).
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge orders Trump administration to pay 'unlawfully' restricted USAID funds
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Amir Ali, a Biden appointee, has ordered the Trump administration to pay out $2 billion in foreign aid owed to contractors for completed work.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House subcommittee hears testimony on school choice
item.description: Some say parents should be able to choose how their children are educated, while others worry redirecting funds could gut public education.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans advance a government funding bill despite Democratic opposition
item.description: House Republicans passed a critical test of their unity on legislation to avoid a partial government shutdown, teeing up a final vote for a measure that keeps federal agencies funded through September.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate GOP frets as Wall Street takes dip over tariffs, talk of recession
item.description: Senate Republicans expressed new worries about the economy Monday after Wall Street had one of its worst days in years amid a trade war and remarks from President Trump that did not rule out the possibility of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kentucky bourbon makers fear becoming 'collateral damage' in Trump's trade war
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — With a new distillery set to open soon, the makers of Brough Brothers bourbon in Kentucky were ready to put their business plan into action. They were looking to ramp up whiskey production to break into lucrative new markets in Canada and Europe.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former state Sen. López Franzen announces U.S. Senate run in Minnesota
item.description: Melisa López Franzen, a former Democratic leader in the Legislature, said she will run for Minnesota’s open U.S. Senate seat next year. She most recently worked at the University of Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Drive to delay paid family and medical leave program stalls in Minnesota House for now
item.description: The proposal would have postponed implementation by one year. The paid family and medical leave program is set to take effect in 2026. A planned vote was the only thing postponed.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump pushes detente between Musk and Rubio
item.description: Elon Musk and Secretary of State Marco Rubio sought to project unity Monday, following a private clash at a recent Cabinet meeting, with President Trump seeking to play mediator.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge halts effort to deport Columbia protest leader, sets Wednesday hearing
item.description: A federal judge on Monday scheduled a Wednesday court hearing for Mahmoud Khalil, a recent Columbia University graduate who was arrested by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), forbidding the government from moving forward with deportation efforts before then.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dow plunges 890 points after Trump declines to rule out recession
item.description: Stocks have fallen steadily since the start of March in response to a string of underwhelming economic data and whipsaw tariff announcements from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, NewsNation

item.title: An office known for enforcing special education is now focused on Trump's political priorities
item.description: The Education Department’s Office for Civil Rights is best known for enforcing disability rights across America’s schools, but under President Donald Trump it’s being reshaped into a frontline figure in the president’s political battles.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley And Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Protestors dispute Vance’s description of clash on street over Ukraine
item.description: In a post on X, Vance said he was confronted by pro-Ukraine protestors while he was with his 3-year-old daughter on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Tribes and Native American students sue over Bureau of Indian Education firings
item.description: Three tribal nations and five Native American students say in a lawsuit that the Trump administration has failed its legal obligations to tribes when it cut jobs at Bureau of Indian Education schools.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
